question_text: For a given data $( x _ { i } , y _ { i } ) , i = 1 , 2 , \ldots , n ,$ with $\textstyle \sum _ { i = 1 } ^ { n } x _ { i } ^ { 2 } > 0 .$ , let $\hat { \beta }$ satisfy $ \sum _ { i = 1 } ^ { n } ( y _ { i } - { \hat { \beta } } x _ { i } ) ^ { 2 } = \operatorname* { i n f } _ { \beta \in \mathbb { R } } \sum _ { i = 1 } ^ { n } ( y _ { i } - \beta x _ { i } ) ^ { 2 } . $ Further, let $v _ { j } = y _ { j } - x _ { j }$ and $u _ { j } = 2 x _ { j }$ , for $j = 1 , 2 , \dots , n$ , and let $\hat { \gamma }$ satisfy $ \sum _ { i = 1 } ^ { n } ( v _ { i } - \hat { \gamma } u _ { i } ) ^ { 2 } = \operatorname* { i n f } _ { \gamma \in \mathbb { R } } \sum _ { i = 1 } ^ { n } ( v _ { i } - \gamma u _ { i } ) ^ { 2 } . $ If ${ \hat { \beta } } = 1 0$ , then the value of $\cdot \hat { \gamma }$ is:
solution: The given problem involves two optimization problems for minimizing the sum of squared errors.
